summary: Matched and linked the green-shell item object `src/melee/it/items/itgshell.c`. The PR made small codegen-oriented edits, factored shared shell-hit behavior into a `static inline` helper, corrected local constant symbol labels, and flipped `itgshell.c` from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in `configure.py`. The decomp.dev bot reported 4 new 100% function matches and GALE01 gains of +1320 matched-code bytes, +5896 linked-code bytes, and +197 linked-data bytes.

key_files.role: Primary matched source; adjusted locals, stack padding, random masking, and shared clank/hitshield logic.
Reclassified several itgshell-adjacent sdata/sdata2 constants from global `it_804D...` names to local `@NNN` labels.

what_changed: `it_8028B8D8` now uses an explicit `f32 temp` for `-ip->facing_dir` before multiplying `v.x`, instead of doing the negated multiply inline.
`it_8028BC2C` now masks `it_804D6D28->x48_byte` through a `u8 rand_max` local with `rand_max &= 0xF` before calling `HSD_Randi(rand_max)`.

The duplicated post-hit shell response sequence from `itGShell_Logic14_Clanked` and `itGShell_Logic14_HitShield` was moved into `static inline void shellHit(HSD_GObj* gobj)` and called from both functions.

`shellHit` contains the shared rebound/reset sequence: `it_802756D0`, `it_80275444`, randomized x velocity, y velocity from attrs, random state update through `xDE8`, `it_802762BC`, `Item_80268E5C(..., ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE)`, `it_80274C88`, and updates to `xDEC_b3` and `xDE4`.
`itGShell_Logic14_HitShield` keeps the shield-bounce branch for `msid == 3 || msid == 4`, and delegates the `ip->msid - 5u <= 3` case to `shellHit`.
`configure.py` now links `melee/it/items/itgshell.c` as a matching object.

`symbols.txt` changed `it_804D5270`, `it_804D5278`, `it_804DCA38`, `it_804DCA3C`, `it_804DCA40`, and `it_804DCA48` from global symbols to local labels `@265`, `@266`, `@168`, `@169`, `@207`, and `@329`.
smart_moves: Used exact-width temporaries, especially `u8 rand_max`, to influence instruction selection and argument preparation for `HSD_Randi` without changing behavior.
Replaced duplicated callback code with a `static inline` helper, preserving callsite inlining while making the source smaller and easier to keep consistent.
Removed stale `PAD_STACK` padding once local declarations and refactoring produced the desired stack layout.
Updated the build configuration only after the object reached matching status, avoiding premature linking of a still-nonmatching item file.
Adjusted symbol scope for compiler-local constants instead of leaving them as misleading exported `it_804D...` globals.
decomp_lessons: Algebraically equivalent expressions can differ in MWCC output; splitting `v.x *= -ip->facing_dir` into a temporary assignment was part of the successful match.
For byte fields used in bitmasks, preserve the apparent intermediate type. `u8 rand_max = it_804D6D28->x48_byte; rand_max &= 0xF;` matched where an inline `x48_byte & 0xF` did not.
`static inline` is useful when two functions share a machine-code sequence but the final object still needs duplicated inlined code rather than an emitted helper call.
Treat `PAD_STACK` as a temporary matching crutch; remove it when real locals/control flow can explain the stack frame.
When a source file becomes matching/linkable, revisit `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` so file-local strings/floats/doubles use local `@NNN` labels instead of global address names.
naming_conventions: Existing exported item callbacks kept the project’s address/name style, such as `it_8028BC2C`, `itGShell_Logic14_Clanked`, and `itGShell_Logic14_HitShield`.
The file already uses mixed `itGShell`/`itGshell` capitalization; this PR preserved names such as `itGshell_UnkMotion6_Anim` rather than renaming for style.
The new helper was file-local lower camelCase, `shellHit`, and marked `static inline` rather than exported.
Local constants in `symbols.txt` use anonymous `@NNN` labels, while true globals retain `it_804D...` address-style names.
Green-shell item state continues to be accessed through `ip->xDD4_itemVar.gshell` fields such as `xDDC`, `xDD8`, `xDE8`, `xDEC_b0`, `xDEC_b3`, and `xDE4`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduce a named `f32` temporary for a negated float member before a vector multiply to match evaluation/register behavior.
Mask a byte value in a `u8` local before passing it to `HSD_Randi`; do not assume an inline bitwise expression will compile identically.
Use `static inline` extraction to share source while still allowing duplicated machine code in the callers.
Remove unnecessary `PAD_STACK` after the true frame shape is recovered.
Convert matched object constants in `.sdata` and `.sdata2` to local `@NNN` symbols so the linked data layout matches.
review_feedback: No human PR body or human review comments were present in the slice.
The automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed matched code at 54.57% (+0.03%, +1320 bytes), linked code at 31.45% (+0.15%, +5896 bytes), and linked data at 28.94% (+0.02%, +197 bytes).
The automated report listed 4 new matches: `itGShell_Logic14_HitShield`, `it_8028BC2C`, `itGshell_UnkMotion6_Anim`, and `itGShell_Logic14_Clanked`, all reaching 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Search remaining item files for `HSD_Randi(... & 0xF)` patterns that may need a `u8` temporary for matching.
Compare `itgshell` shell-hit/clank logic with `itrshell` or other shell item files for similar `static inline` matching opportunities.
Audit item-object `it_804D...` constants in `symbols.txt` after future matches to identify globals that should become local `@NNN` labels.
Look for remaining `PAD_STACK` shims in nearly matched item callbacks that can be removed by recovering locals or control-flow structure.
